acceptedQuestions.subject: Science
acceptedQuestions.prompt: The term 'Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP)', sometimes seen in the news in the context of the drug Lenacapavir, best describes:

options.text: a post-exposure vaccine administered immediately after suspected contact with infected body fluids
the use of antiretroviral medications by uninfected individuals to prevent the acquisition of a viral infection
a targeted therapeutic regimen administered exclusively to pregnant women to prevent mother-to-child transmission of viruses
a specialized gene therapy aimed at restoring destroyed CD4 T lymphocytes in immunodeficient patients

acceptedQuestions.explanation: Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) involves the proactive use of antiretroviral medicines to prevent HIV infection in individuals who are at risk but not yet infected. Lenacapavir is a recently approved antiretroviral medicine utilized for this preventive purpose.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: PrEP is a preventive strategy for uninfected individuals, distinct from post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP) or therapeutic treatments (like ART) meant for those already infected.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: FDA approved Lenacapavir, an antiretroviral medicine used for HIV prevention as Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP).
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Excellent conceptual question. The distractors are highly plausible as they cleverly reuse vocabulary from the source text (body fluids, mother-to-child, CD4 T lymphocytes) to create incorrect definitions.

reviewerNotes: Selected Candidate 1 for testing factual knowledge about HIV transmission, mechanism, and the new drug Lenacapavir. Selected Candidate 4 for testing the conceptual understanding of the term 'PrEP' using strong, text-grounded distractors. Both questions have been refined to match UPSC standards in tone and formatting.
